text:
Once upon a summer afternoon...

There thrived a curious little bird named Clover. Clover lived among a forest of vibrant orange blossoms. Each morning she flew far enough to explore, discovering flowers so sweet they seemed to hum whispers of spring. One day, curious about the forest's secrets further than ever before, Clover wingsfluttered towards a whisper said to carry enchantment.

It was a hollow oak hollowed by time itself. Something was happening within. Clover listened intently.

The whispers grew louder. She listened closer.

A melody so sweet flowed from the trunk. Voices? Clover knew they weren't human. They sung of mysteries old.

An owl? Perhaps...

Clovero flew closer and listened intently.

Owl voices!

That's when Clover noticed a nest. Nestlings peeking from their sleep.

She gently landed near them. They blinked curious eyes.

That tiny visitor was more than brave—it felt wise!

The nestlings chirped hello.

Clover shared tales. They listened eagerly
